title: Rayleigh fading mitigation via short pulse coherent distributed acoustic sensing with multi-location beating-term combination
abstract: Aspects of the present disclosure describe Rayleigh fading mitigation via short pulse coherent distributed acoustic sensing with multi-location beating-term combination. In illustrative configurations, systems, methods, and structures according to the present disclosure employ a two stage modulation arrangement providing short interrogator pulses resulting in a greater number of sensing data points and reduced effective sectional length. The increased number of data points are used to mitigate Rayleigh fading via a spatial combining process, multi-location-beating combining (MLBC) which uses weighted complex-valued DAS beating results from neighboring locations and aligns phase signals of each of the locations, before combining them to produce a final DAS phase measurement. Since Rayleigh scattering is a random statistic, the MLBC process allows capture of different statics from neighboring locations with correlated vibration/acoustic signal. The combined DAS results minimize a total Rayleigh fade, in both dynamic fading and static fading scenarios.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. An improved optical fiber distributed acoustic sensing system comprising: a length of optical fiber; and an optical interrogator unit that generates optical pulses, introduces them into the optical fiber and receives Rayleigh reflected signals from the fiber; the improved optical fiber sensing system CHARACTERIZED BY : a coherent receiver unit configured to extract information from the Rayleigh reflected signals; and said short-pulse interrogation optical pulses having a pulse-width <= 4ns. 2. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 1 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: a two stage, cascaded pulse modulator arrangement. 3. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 2 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: a multi-polarization-state (MPSC) combining process. 4. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 3 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the MPSC process includes four beating products z cc , zn> , z c , zn c that are computed at once in a digital signal processor (DSP), and all of them are used for fiber strain computation. 5. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 4 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the four beating products z cc , zn>, z c > , zn c are weighted and combined into a single combined beat product result, z[h]. 6. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 1 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: a common (same) laser source is used in generating interrogator pulses and as a local oscillator (LO) for the coherent receiver. 7. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 2 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the first stage of the two stage cascaded modulator arrangement is a Mach-Zehnder modulator (MZM). 8. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 7 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the second stage of the two stage cascaded modulator arrangement is a modulator selected from the group consisting of acousto-optic modulator (AOM), a semiconductor optical amplifier (SOA) switch, and Mach-Zehnder modulator. 9. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 2 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the first stage Mach-Zehnder modulator is configured with a bias voltage set to minimum transmittance, using an automatic bias controller (ABC). 10. The improved optical fiber sensing system of claim 2 FURTHER CHARACTERIZED BY: the two stage cascaded modulator arrangement is a single SOA switch one or more electronic filters for removing out-of-band noise signals.
